title: धन्वन्तरि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री धन्वन्तरि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

प्रथमं धन्वन्तरिं नाम द्वितीयं आयुर्वेददम्।
तृतीयं अमृतकलशधरं चतुर्थं सुधाकरम्॥१॥

पञ्चमं वैद्यराजं च षष्ठं दिवोदासं विदुः।
सप्तमं आरोग्यकारिणं अष्टमं सर्वरोगहरम्॥२॥

नवमं विष्णुअवतारं दशमं अमृतोद्भवम्।
एकादशं जरामृत्युहरं द्वादशं प्राणरक्षकम्॥३॥

द्वादशैतानि नामानि धन्वन्तरेर्महात्मनः।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वरोगैः प्रमुच्यते॥४॥

दीर्घायुष्यं लभेत्तस्य निरामयं च जीवनम्।
धन्वन्तरिकृपा तस्य सदा आरोग्यदायिनी॥५॥

॥ इति श्री धन्वन्तरि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram ||

Prathamam Dhanvantarim Nama Dvitiyam Ayurveda-dam.
Tritiyam Amrita-kalasha-dharam Chaturtham Sudha-karam.||1||

Panchamam Vaidya-rajam Cha Shashtham Divodasam Viduh.
Saptamam Arogya-karinam Ashtamam Sarva-roga-haram.||2||

Navamam Vishnu-avataram Dashamam Amritodbhavam.
Ekadasham Jara-mrityu-haram Dvadasham Prana-rakshakam.||3||

Dvadashai-tani Namani Dhanvantarer-mahatmanah.
Yah Pathet Pratar-utthaya Sarva-rogaih Pramuchyate.||4||

Dirghayushyam Labhet-tasya Niramayam Cha Jivanam.
Dhanvantari-kripa Tasya Sada Arogya-dayini.||5||

|| Iti Shri Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: प्रथम नाम धन्वन्तरि, दूसरा आयुर्वेदद, तीसरा अमृतकलशधर, चौथा सुधाकर। पाँचवाँ वैद्यराज, छठा दिवोदास, सातवाँ आरोग्यकारी, आठवाँ सर्वरोगहर। नवाँ विष्णुअवतार, दसवाँ अमृतोद्भव, ग्यारहवाँ जरामृत्युहर, बारहवाँ प्राणरक्षक। धन्वन्तरि के इन बारह नामों का प्रातःकाल पाठ करने से सभी रोगों से मुक्ति मिलती है, दीर्घायु प्राप्त होती है और निरामय जीवन होता है।
benefits: Twelve names of Lord Dhanvantari, the divine physician
Associated in tradition with all diseases and health ailments when recited daily
Invokes the blessing of long life and disease-free living
Invokes the healing avatar of Lord Vishnu
Ideal for patients, healthcare workers, and Ayurveda practitioners
description: The Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram, known in Sanskrit as धन्वन्तरि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Dhanvantari. Attributed to Bhagavata Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to twelve names of Lord Dhanvantari, the divine physician as well as invoke the healing avatar of Lord Vishnu. Additionally, it is known to cure all diseases and health ailments when recited daily. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Dhanteras and Dhanvantari Jayanti for enhanced blessings. With 5 verses and a reading time of about 3 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram (धन्वन्तरि द्वादश नाम स्तोत्रम्) is a short Sanskrit hymn enumerating the twelve sacred names of Bhagavan Dhanvantari, the divine physician (Vaidya-raja) and Vishnu-avatara who emerged from the Samudra Manthan (churning of the ocean of milk) holding the Amrita Kalasha — the pot of nectar of immortality. The twelve names recited are Dhanvantari, Ayurveda-da, Amrita-kalasha-dhara, Sudha-kara, Vaidya-raja, Divodasa, Arogya-karina, Sarva-roga-hara, Vishnu-avatara, Amritodbhava, Jara-mrityu-hara, and Prana-rakshaka. Each name encodes one healing aspect of the Lord — giver of Ayurveda, holder of the nectar pot, king of physicians, remover of all disease, conqueror of old age and death, protector of life-breath. The phala-shruti promises long life, freedom from disease and a niramaya (illness-free) existence to those who recite the names at dawn. (Source: classical Dhanvantari devotional tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Dhanvantari and what is his connection with Ayurveda?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Dhanvantari is the divine physician (Devata-vaidya) and the avatara of Vishnu who arose from the Kshira-sagara during the Samudra Manthan, holding the Amrita Kalasha in one hand. The Bhagavata Purana, Vishnu Purana and Agni Purana describe him as the dispeller of disease and old age. Tradition identifies him with the Vedic king Divodasa Dhanvantari of Kashi (Varanasi), who is hailed in the Sushruta Samhita as the founder-teacher (adi-guru) of Ayurveda surgery (shalya-tantra), having transmitted the Ayurvedic science to Sushruta and his disciples on the banks of the Ganga. Dhanvantari is therefore worshipped as the patron deity of all physicians, surgeons, Ayurveda practitioners, pharmacists and patients seeking healing. His Jayanti falls on Dhanteras (Kartik Krishna Trayodashi), now celebrated as National Ayurveda Day in India. (Sources: Sushruta Samhita; Bhagavata Purana 8.8; Agni Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram is most powerful on Dhanteras / Dhanvantari Jayanti (Kartik Krishna Trayodashi — the official birthday of the Lord and National Ayurveda Day), Akshaya Tritiya, Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Sunday (Ravivar). The phala-shruti specifies prataruthaya (immediately on rising from bed in the morning) as the ideal recitation time, before any food or speech. Daily recitation in the morning facing east, before a Dhanvantari image with a tulsi mala, is the practice in many Ayurveda gurukulas, vaidya families and pharmacies across Kerala, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka. The stotra is also recited by patients and family members of patients during illness, before surgery, while taking medicine, and during the Mritunjaya-homa for terminal disease. (Sources: Dhanvantari upasana tradition; Ayurveda Vidya Parishat.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti of the Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram promises the following blessings to sincere daily recitation: (1) sarva-rogaih pramuchyate — liberation from all diseases of body, mind and prana, (2) dirghayushyam — long life, (3) niramayam jivanam — a healthy, illness-free existence, (4) Dhanvantari-kripa — the personal grace of the divine physician, (5) sada arogya — perpetual health and vitality, (6) protection from epidemics, viral fevers, chronic conditions, surgery complications and side-effects of medication, (7) success in Ayurvedic and allopathic treatment, (8) blessings for vaidyas, surgeons and pharmacists in their practice. The stotra is especially recommended for patients with chronic illness, those undergoing surgery, families with elderly members, and households facing recurrent illness. (Source: Dhanvantari devotional corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve names of Lord Dhanvantari listed in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The twelve sacred names of Lord Dhanvantari are: (1) Dhanvantari — the original name, holder of the bow (dhanus) of healing, (2) Ayurveda-da — the giver of Ayurveda, (3) Amrita-kalasha-dhara — the holder of the pot of immortality nectar, (4) Sudha-kara — the maker of nectar, (5) Vaidya-raja — the king of physicians, (6) Divodasa — the Vedic king-sage of Kashi who taught Ayurveda surgery, (7) Arogya-karina — the giver of perfect health, (8) Sarva-roga-hara — the remover of every disease, (9) Vishnu-avatara — the incarnation of Vishnu, (10) Amritodbhava — the one born from the nectar churning, (11) Jara-mrityu-hara — the conqueror of old age and death, and (12) Prana-rakshaka — the protector of the life-breath. Each name is a complete healing meditation. (Source: Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama and the Dhanvantari Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are Dhanvantari upasana texts but differ in form and use. The Dhanvantari Mantra is the traditional dhyana mantra Om Namo Bhagavate Maha-Sudarshana Vasudevaya Dhanvantaraye Amrita-kalasha-hastaya Sarva-amaya-vinashanaya Trailokya-nathaya Sri Maha-Vishnave Namah — a single, dense bija-rich mantra used for japa, homa and abhisheka, especially in Ayurveda hospitals and during Dhanvantari Homa for terminal illness. The Dhanvantari Dwadash Nama Stotram is a longer 5-verse hymn enumerating the twelve names of the Lord with a phala-shruti promising long life and freedom from disease — used as a daily devotional path-recitation rather than as a japa mantra. Many vaidyas recite the mantra 108 times and the Dwadash Nama Stotram once daily. Both are part of the standard Dhanvantari upasana paddhati. (Source: classical Dhanvantari upasana tradition.)
